question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: call()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that throttleManager.throttle(request) consumes tokens from both rateLimitBucket and gasLimitBucket atomically, but the restore only happens for InvalidParametersException, to craft a sequence of requests that each pass throttle but fail in constructServiceParameters() with a non-InvalidParametersException, causing permanent token loss and bucket depletion?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: constructServiceParameters()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with the from field omitted (null) causing Address.ZERO to be used as sender, combined with a high value field, to bypass the hasFrom() validation check (which only requires from != null when value > 0) and trigger unexpected EVM execution paths that consume excessive resources?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: call()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with estimate=true targeting a contract address that causes the binary search gas estimation to always fail (e.g., a contract that always reverts), forcing the maximum number of retry iterations (maxGasEstimateRetriesCount=20) on every request, consuming 20x the normal server resources and griefing other users?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: call()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests that trigger the requestTimeout (10000ms default) by crafting EVM calls that execute just under the timeout threshold, holding server threads for the maximum duration and reducing the effective throughput of the server, causing other users' requests to queue up and eventually time out?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: call()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the gas field defaults to 15000000 when not provided in the JSON body, meaning any request without an explicit gas field automatically consumes the maximum gas tokens from the gasLimitBucket, allowing an attacker to send minimal-payload requests that each drain maximum throttle capacity?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: call()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a carefully crafted data field that triggers EVM CALL opcodes to many different contract addresses, causing a large number of database lookups per single request (one per called contract), saturating the database connection pool and degrading service for all other users?
